core_loop.one_sentence: Deploy units into your moving castle to destroy the enemy's moving castle before yours is destroyed.
core_loop.player_goal: Reduce the enemy castle's health to 0%.
core_loop.failure_or_pressure: The enemy castle constantly fires back, depleting your health.
core_loop.why_it_is_fun: Satisfying destruction physics and the strategic choice of which units to deploy.

player_controls.target: Unit icon in bottom UI, then empty slot in the castle
player_controls.result: Unit is placed in the slot and begins auto-attacking

visible_ui.purpose: Show health of player (blue) and enemy (red) castles
Allow player to choose which unit to deploy

timeline_beats.playable_relevance: Hook the player immediately with action

mechanics.name: Unit Deployment
mechanics.description: Placing units into designated slots to attack.

mechanics.name: Auto-Battling
mechanics.description: Placed units and castles automatically fire projectiles.

mechanics.name: Destruction Physics
mechanics.description: Castles visually break apart as health decreases.

feedback_and_juice.visual_or_audio_feedback: Explosions, smoke, flying debris, damage numbers

can_simplify: Complex physics destruction (use simple sprite swapping or particle bursts)

variation_hooks.effect: Increases or decreases difficulty and urgency

variation_hooks.safe_range_or_values: Melee vs Ranged vs Heavy damage

open_questions_or_assumptions: Is there a cost to deploy units, or is it purely slot-based?
Do the castles actually move closer to each other, or is it just an animation effect?
What are the specific differences between the unit types shown?
